pattern: A depth-first walk that prepends each node's letter builds every leaf-to-root string, and tracking the lexicographically smallest completed one yields the answer with simple string comparison.
hints: Each candidate answer is read from a leaf up to the root, so build the string by prefixing the current node's letter as you descend.
Only strings that finish at a leaf are valid candidates; compare them lexicographically.
Recurse carrying the path of letters from the root reversed so far; at each leaf form the full string and keep the minimum across all leaves.

edge_inputs.description: a shorter path can be smaller than a longer one
single node is a one-letter string
left-skewed chain gives one long string
missing child so only some leaves exist
tie at first letter broken deeper

stress.description: random tree of small letter values with holes
stress.note: first element non-null guarantees a root; small alphabet creates ties
